title: Has Anyone Actually Found a Long-Term Profitable EA, or Is It All Overfitting?
author: Annual-Register-3683
selftext:
I’ve been building and testing EAs for a while now, from basic MA crossovers to more complex ideas, and honestly I’m still struggling to find one that holds up long term. Backtests look great, then a few months into live trading the edge slowly fades or disappears. I even run them on tradingfx VPS so execution and uptime aren’t the issue, but the strategy itself still seems to break over time.

It’s made me wonder if I’m chasing something unrealistic, or if I’m just missing the right approach. Have any of you actually run an EA that stayed profitable for a year or more in real conditions? What helped most with robustness testing or adapting to changing markets? And do you think fully automated trading can really work on its own, or does it always need some human oversight?

Genuinely curious to hear real experiences, not sales pitches.

comments.author: Cylosmagic
comments.body: Ive got one on MYFXBOOK Thts been running around 9 months. But ive had in ruining for 2 years on live accounts -it’s low risk under 5% drawdown makes around 5% on average a month also nothing crazy but very reliable

comments.author: EndlessKnight_154
comments.body:
Yup, backtests often look great, but live conditions expose weaknesses. That doesn’t mean you’re doing it wrong; most long-term profitable EAs are simple, low-risk, and adjusted when markets shift.

Even the best systems usually need some human oversight. I run mine too on cheapforex VPS to avoid tech issues, but the real edge comes from strategy, risk management, and knowing when to step back. Fully automated trading can work, but only with careful supervision.

comments.author: sofiarodb14
comments.body: Good point on VPS stability being key for trading. For global deployment, I've found Lightnode's many datacenters super useful.

comments.author: Spirited_Syllabub488
comments.body:
I have my own ML Model. Currently I am looking to sale it for acquiring more funds for my account.  
I have 5 years of report that shows \~10% of average monthly return.  
It is developed to trade BTC and Gold....

Sharpe ratio is >2

This is the report - [https://drive.google.com/file/d/1f\_JYwl4p6iGsYc-gB4h9W3Y5KiJjt--z/view?usp=drive\_link](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1f_JYwl4p6iGsYc-gB4h9W3Y5KiJjt--z/view?usp=drive_link)

If you find it worth it feel free to message me.

replies.author: asis2014
replies.body: How does it works , I'll be interested if you set it up

replies.author: Spirited_Syllabub488
replies.body: It’s a machine-learning driven probability based predictive system that continuously retrains on new market data to adapt to regime and volatility shifts. It is not a high-frequency or quick-scalp strategy; the model operates on higher-timeframe data and predicts next move with old data and the learning the model gets on it. And then the learned weight is used on the present data to evaluate the next possible move. It has a win rate of \~69%.

replies.author: Spirited_Syllabub488
replies.body: We can talk on chat if you find it interesting.

comments.author: Mitzesq
comments.body:
Found one? No.   
Built? Yes, almost.  
Like someone says, it is very hard to anticipate every scenario. Small SL helps alot.

comments.author: ea-forextrading
comments.body:
Have you tried XAUBOT to create your own bot yet? I’ve been using it for more than 2 years in a fully automated setup, I run it on a VPS and once a week, log in to make sure everything is working properly. I also monitor the trades from my mobile phone mt5

Some people expect very high monthly returns and use aggressive settings that don’t match their capital, which can definitely lead to losses. but with XAUBOT I focus on proper risk management, and that’s why I’m comfortable and confident with around 2–5% monthly returns.

comments.author: Backtester4Ever
comments.body: I see this constantly when testing in WealthLab. Robust systems survive multiple market environments, trade less than you expect, and spend long periods doing nothing. Fully automated trading can work, but only if the logic includes regime awareness and hard stop conditions. If an EA needs constant rescue, it was never robust in the first place.

comments.body: more info in new want to get into this
